claim: Exosomal PD-L1—including tumor-derived exosomal PD-L1 in melanoma—binds PD-1 on PD-1+ antigen-experienced CD8 T cells.

claim: Engagement of PD-1 by PD-L1—either on tumor cell surfaces or on tumor-derived exosomes (including in melanoma)—inhibits PD-1+ CD8 T cells by suppressing proliferation, cytokine production, and cytotoxicity, thereby inactivating them.
question: Does PD-L1 engagement of PD-1 inhibit PD-1+ CD8 T cells?

claim: In metastatic melanoma, PD-1 is expressed on T cells.
question: In metastatic melanoma, is PD-1 expressed on T cells?

claim: Anti-PD-1 and anti-PD-L1 antibodies disrupt PD-L1–PD-1 binding on exosomes and block exosomal PD-L1–mediated T-cell inhibition.

question: Does interferon-gamma treatment increase exosome binding to PD-1?
